steps.title: Compare the means
steps.content:
The original sum from the frequency table is $1{,}175$, so the original mean is about $16.55$. Adding $39$ gives a new sum of $1{,}214$ for $72$ tortoises.
$$\frac{1{,}175}{71}=16.55$$
$$\frac{1{,}214}{72}=16.86$$
The mean increases.

steps.title: Find the original median
steps.content: With $71$ values, the median is the $36$th value. The cumulative count reaches $32$ by weight $16$ and $41$ by weight $17$, so the original median is $17$.

steps.title: Find the new median
steps.content: The added value $39$ is the largest, so it becomes the last value. With $72$ values, the median is the average of the $36$th and $37$th values, and both are still $17$. The mean increases, and the medians are equal. The answer is B.
